title: Statistical Consulting for Agricultural, Environmental, and Biological Research
text: I help research teams design experiments, analyze complex biological datasets, and communicate results clearly.

title: About
text: Celeste Falcon is a biostatistician with experience in agricultural research (plant breeding/quantitative genetics, biologicals discovery & development) and applied statistical consulting. She holds a PhD in Applied Plant Sciences from the University of Minnesota, Twin Cities and a BS in Plant Science from Cornell University. Previously, she was a biostatistician at Bayer Crop Science and Ginkgo Bioworks.

title: Portfolio
text:
Independent biostatistician work

Project for client A - Statistical testing, data interpretation, and report generation for scientific studies focused on the effects of a pesticide on larval mosquitoes.

Publications

Postdoctoral research: Falcon CM, Kaeppler SM, Spalding EP, et al. Relative utility of agronomic, phenological, and morphological traits for assessing genotype-by-environment interaction in maize inbreds. Crop Science. 2020;60:62–81. https://doi.org/10.1002/csc2.20035
Graduate research: Falcon, C.M., Horsley, R., Hu, G., Blake, T. and Smith, K.P. (2019), Mapping QTLs for Grain Protein Concentration and Agronomic Traits under Different Nitrogen Levels in Barley. Crop Science, 59: 68-83. https://doi.org/10.2135/cropsci2018.03.0208
